item.title: The electromechanical angle computer inside the B-52 bomber's star tracker 摘要
item.description:
Before GPS, aircraft used celestial navigation by tracking stars, planets, or the sun for accurate, unjammable positioning. In the early 1960s, the B-52 bomber automated this with the Astro Compass system, which included an Astro Tracker to lock onto stars and an electromechanical Angle Computer that modeled the celestial sphere to compute navigation data. The system used spherical trigonometry via a navigational triangle to convert global star coordinates from an Air Almanac into local azimuth and altitude angles, providing headings accurate to a tenth of a degree.
标签: navigation, aviation, history, technology, celestial-navigation, B-52, analog-computing, electromechanical, Astro-Compass, military

item.title: Mac Mini and Mac Studio Supply Shortages 摘要
item.description:
The article reports on significant supply shortages for Apple's Mac Mini and Mac Studio desktop computers, particularly models with high RAM configurations (32GB+). These AI-friendly systems are currently unavailable or have extended shipping delays of up to 12 weeks, while other Mac models like the MacBook Pro remain readily available. Analysts attribute the shortages to a surge in demand from new 'desktop AI' systems that are currently exclusive to macOS, combined with Apple potentially tapering production ahead of anticipated M5-generation releases. The situation highlights the Mac's unique position as the preferred platform for early adopters of desktop AI applications.
标签: Apple, Mac, hardware, supply-chain, AI, desktop-computing, Mac-Mini, Mac-Studio, RAM, shortages

item.title: ★ ‘A Reading Room on Wheels, a Lover’s Lane, and, After 11 PM, a Flophouse’ 摘要
item.description:
A collection of 18 previously unseen early photographs by filmmaker Stanley Kubrick, taken in the 1940s for Look magazine, will debut at the Photography Show in New York. The images, focusing on New York subway scenes, capture candid moments like sleeping passengers and late-night activities. Kubrick used natural light to preserve the subway's mood, shooting at 1/8 second with a Contax camera during late-night rides over two weeks. The photos highlight his observational skill and offer a historical glimpse into subway life, with parallels to modern commuting experiences.
标签: Stanley Kubrick, photography, New York subway, historical photos, Look magazine, documentary, film director, 1940s, candid photography, art exhibition

item.title: A Taxing Discussion 摘要
item.description:
The article explores the historical origins of the U.S. income tax and Form 1040, introduced in 1913 after the Sixteenth Amendment. Initially targeting only high earners (over $3,000, equivalent to $100,000 today), it became widespread during World War I, with top rates reaching 77% for millionaires. Early confusion and media guidance, like from B.C. Forbes and The New York Times, highlighted the form's complexity. The piece connects this to broader themes of taxation evolution and public adaptation.
标签: taxes, history, US history, income tax, Form 1040, World War I, economics, policy, legal history, media

item.title: Tiny Go and Rust programs appear to start equally fast (on some machines) 摘要
item.description:
The author tested startup time differences between 'hello world' programs in Rust, Go, C, and Python. On FreeBSD, Rust and Go startup times were similar (within microseconds), while C was slightly faster. On Linux, Go startup was about twice as slow as Rust (millisecond difference). Statically linked executables were faster than dynamically linked ones across languages. The author concludes Rust has less runtime startup overhead on Linux but not FreeBSD, and notes that language choice depends on specific performance needs and OS environment.
标签: programming, performance, Rust, Go, benchmarking, startup-time, systems-programming, FreeBSD, Linux, C
